items.title: Planned wind farm linked to Tasmanian minister has some residents worried

children.content: Despite the links being declared, some locals worry politicians with a financial stake in a planned development in Tasmania's north will have little sympathy for their concerns about the project.

items.title: 'Large quantity of arrows' fired in suburbs after archery club theft, police say

children.content: Tasmania Police recover bows and arrows, and arrest and charge two youths over an alleged burglary at a Burnie archery club.

image.altText: A man holds a compound bow in a forest setting.

items.title: Body found after house fire in north-west Tasmania

children.content: A woman in her 60s is believed to be the victim of a fatal house fire in Tasmania's north-west early this morning, firefighters say.

image.altText: A white home with black marks where a fire has charred the roof.

items.title: Nightly news bulletin to move from Launceston after 64 years

children.content: Nightly television news bulletins will no longer be broadcast from Launceston after Seven confirmed that 7NEWS Tasmania will relocate to a studio in Hobart.

image.altText: A hand holding a microphone. Foam on top of the microphone has '7 News' on it.

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

image.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: There are more e-bikes on the road. So what are Tasmania's rules?

children.content: The Bicycle Network explains the rules for using an e-bike legally, including how insurance works and what sort of visibility is required.

gallery.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

thumbnail.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

items.title: Artemis II chases record for the farthest humans have ventured from Earth

children.content: The Apollo 13 crew has held the distance record for a crewed aircraft's maximum range from Earth since the 1970s. The Artemis II crew is about to change that.

image.altText: A view of the moon from the Orion space capsule captured by the Artemis II crew.

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

image.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.

items.title: 'I hate points': Gunston's last-second behind helps Hawthorn claim thriller

children.content: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston kicks a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

image.altText: Jack Gunston hugs Mitch Lewis after Hawthorn's win

items.title: Mayhem at Murray River race as ski boat launches into tree

children.content: Flips, spin-outs and crashes have sent four competitors to hospital as the world's fastest water ski race was held without skiers for the second straight year after fatalities in the sport in 2024.

image.altText: A speedboat turning a corner at speed in a river.

gallery.altText: Dozens of people on water ski boats at a river bank, with the Murray River in the background

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of people on water ski boats at a river bank, with the Murray River in the background

items.title: Push to axe Kanye West from Wireless Festival after past antisemitic remarks

children.content: There is increasing pressure for the rapper to be pulled as headliner of the Wireless Festival in London, as UK PM Keir Starmer weighs in and Pepsi withdraws as lead sponsor.

image.altText: Kanye West performing on stage holding microphone in hand

items.title: No Bluesfest? No worries as stars come out to wow Byron Easter crowds

children.content: There was no Bluesfest, but thousands of music fans still packed venues across the Byron Shire over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A concert with lots of people holding up their phones

items.title: The unexpected place new collectors are finding affordable art

children.content: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

image.altText: Sonja Bowden, a middle-aged blonde woman, smiles, holding a wine, beside a drawing of a dog, with a "First place" label.

items.title: Bic Runga thought her first album in 15 years might never arrive

children.content: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

image.altText: Bic Runga standing outside an art studio at night, illuminated by colourful window displays.

items.title: Three easy sausage dinners that don't involve mash and peas

children.content: If you want some new ways to use sausages, these three easy Italian-inspired recipes will get you excited about them.

gallery.altText: A tray with baked potato slices, crushed tinned tomato, capsicum, pork sausages and oregano, a oven baked family dinner.

thumbnail.altText: A tray with baked potato slices, crushed tinned tomato, capsicum, pork sausages and oregano, a oven baked family dinner.

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

gallery.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it
A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

thumbnail.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it
A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

items.title: Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel

children.content: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

image.altText: A large cross and a flag of Lebanon erected on top of hill, underneath large letters read Rmeich

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

image.altText: retirees holding placards

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

gallery.altText: A imagery with graphics effect showing a young girl in a pink sweater. Behind is a focus on an undentified craft over an oval

thumbnail.altText: A imagery with graphics effect showing a young girl in a pink sweater. Behind is a focus on an undentified craft over an oval

items.title: Up to five on the run after smashing car into Brisbane luxury store

children.content: Police say a Mercedes SUV was deliberately driven into the store in the early hours today.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a store with a smashed in door.

items.title: Man found dead after being swept away in floodwaters in SA's far north

children.content: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A man looks into the camera. He has white hair and is wearing a hat.

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

gallery.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

thumbnail.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

items.title: Melbourne woman Eva Lasrini remembered as devoted mother of two

children.content: A Dandenong woman whose body was found south-west of Melbourne on Saturday has been remembered as a devoted mother who "deserved a life filled with safety, happiness, and peace".

image.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.

items.title: US star Sha'Carri Richardson overcomes semis scare to win Stawell Gift

children.content: American sprint star Sha'Carri Richardson has become the third woman ever to win the famous Stawell Gift from scratch.

image.altText: Sha'Carri Richardson poses after winning a sprint heat

items.title: Graham Arnold mobbed by Iraq fans celebrating World Cup qualification

children.content: Former Socceroos coach has received a hero's welcome at Sydney Airport after helping Iraq qualify for its first FIFA World Cup in 40 years.

image.altText: Iraq men's coach Graham Arnold is surrounded by police and Iraq football fans as he smiles and leaves the airport terminal.

items.title: How selectors hope to handle Australia's aging team ahead of away Ashes

children.content: Australia's aging men's cricket side is not about to "drop off" in performance, but the chairman of selectors says expectations should be managed as the squad goes through a change in the near future.

image.altText: Australian selector George Bailey talking to two people at cricket nets

items.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru

children.content: Bruce Munro's  art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

gallery.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
Vibrant reds, oranges and purples cover the ground under a night sky, while in the fore tourists take photos of each other.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene

thumbnail.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
Vibrant reds, oranges and purples cover the ground under a night sky, while in the fore tourists take photos of each other.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene

items.title: 16yo surfer feels 'lucky' to have foot after SA shark attack

children.content: Oliver Tokic-Bensley says he was sitting on his surfboard alone at Middleton Beach when he said he felt something grab his foot.
Warning: This story contains images some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: The back of a 16-year-old boy's head as he holds his surfboard in front of him while looking out at the water.

items.title: Vet helping native bat pups has 'never seen mass casualties like this'

children.content: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

image.altText: A volunteer feeds a rescued baby flying fox, which is swaddled in a colourful blanket.

items.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for young Aboriginal rangers

children.content: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

image.altText: A man holding a bag out of which a small warru black footed rock wallaby has jumped out of

items.title: Fire-affected towns welcome Easter visitors

children.content: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

image.altText: A smiling man in a shirt and cap leans on a farm fence

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations booming across sanctuaries

children.content: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

A small marsupial is held by one person as another person conducts a health check.
A small marsupial being held in a dark blue bag.
